title: Bytes, Object Representation, Alignment, Padding, and Endianness · Memory Retrieval
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the portable binary representation codec contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for portable binary representation codec after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect object representations through character types while separating value representation, padding, and trap risks.
Compute and satisfy fundamental, extended, member, and allocated alignment requirements before access.
Measure rather than assume member offsets, tail padding, and layout changes across ABI targets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
